title: Enumeration type names should avoid having Flags or Enum suffixes
description: Enumeration type names should not have "Flags" or "Enum" suffixesAnalyze your codeconsistency-identifiablemaintainabilityCode SmellconventionWhy is this an issue?The information that an enumeration type is actually an enumeration or a set of flags should not be duplicated in its name.Noncompliant code exampleenum FooFlags // Noncompliant { Foo = 1 Bar = 2 Baz = 4 }Compliant solutionenum Foo { Foo = 1 Bar = 2 Baz = 4 }Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CIAvailable Since9.1Analyze code in youron-premise CIDeveloper EditionAvailable Since9.1©2025SonarSource Sàrl. All rights reserved.Privacy Policy|Cookie Policy|Terms of Use
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.detection_conditions: Enum type names with 'Flags' or 'Enum' suffix
logic.never_trigger_on: Not applicable - Flags/Enum suffixes are always redundant
logic.reasoning: Enum type names should not include 'Flags' or 'Enum' suffix as the type system already indicates it's an enum. The [Flags] attribute indicates flags without needing it in the name. Pattern detects 'enum NameFlags' or 'enum NameEnum' declarations.
